title: Review x-auto guardrail gaps for medical/AI-diagnostic posts lacking primary-source links. Focus on /Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/approval_guard.py, post_rules.py, scheduler.py, and related tests. Need: (1) identify bypasses caused by note/x quote exemptions, (2) propose the minimal safe code change an...
summary_text: Coverage gaps I found: 1. Missing direct interactive slash-command coverage (`/vote` and `/v`) in snippet test - In [`test-codex-orchestrator-snippet.sh`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-codex-orchestrator-snippet.sh:41), all paths are exercised via `codex ...`/`codex exec ...`, but there is no `zsh -lc 'source ...; /vote ...'` or `...; /v ...'` assertion. - Concrete missing assertions: - `/vote foo` from sourced interactive zsh calls launcher path (or fallback) and logs `prompt-launch vote foo`. - `/v foo` from sourced interactive zsh logs `prompt-launch v foo`. - Neither invocation falls back to `raw-codex` or `kernel` for these slash prompts. 2. Missing `/v` parity check under `codex exec` slash routing - There is a `/vote` check ([line 88])(/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-codex-orchestrator-snippet.sh:88), but no equivalent `codex exec -C ... "/v ..."` assertion. - Concrete missing assertions: - `codex exec -C <repo> "/v TOKEN=..."` logs `prompt-launch v TOKEN=...`. - `raw-codex exec ... "/v ..."` is not invoked. 3. Missing negative assertion for `/vote` codex-exec path - Current `/vote` case only checks positive `prompt-launch vote ...` ([line 90])(/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-codex-orchestrator-snippet.sh:90). - Concrete missing assertions: - `raw-codex exec -C ... "/vote ..."` must not appear. - `kernel ...` must not appear for `/vote`/`/v` non-kernel payloads. 4. `test-vote-launchers.sh` does not test interactive-zsh entrypoint behavior at all - It invokes binaries directly (`"${HOME}/bin/vote"`, `"${HOME}/bin/v"`) at [lines 30-31](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-vote-launchers.sh:30), so direct `/vote` and `/v` interactive function routing from `codex-orchestrator.zsh` is unverified. - Concrete missing assertions: - `source codex-orchestrator.zsh` then `/vote focus-one` reaches expected launcher. - same for `/v focus-two`. 5. Missing assertion that launcher hint is called with correct subcommand - Launcher scripts call `orchestrator-entrypoint-hint vote|v` ([`vote`: line 5](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/launchers/vote:5), [`v`: line 5](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/launchers/v:5)), but current test only checks final `codex-prompt-launch` args. - Concrete missing assertions: - log contains `hint vote` before `vote focus-one`. - log contains `hint v` before `v focus-two`. 6. Missing resilience assertion when hint fails (because script uses `|| true`) - `test-vote-launchers.sh` uses hint stub that always exits 0 ([lines 11-14](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-vote-launchers.sh:11)). - Concrete missing assertions: - with hint exiting non-zero, `vote` and `v` still call `codex-prompt-launch` and exit successfully. 7. Missing argument-preservation assertions for multi-token payloads - Current checks use single-token payloads (`focus-one`, `focus-two`) ([lines 30-34](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-vote-launchers.sh:30)). - Concrete missing assertions: - multi-word args survive as separate args through launcher (`vote a b "c d"` → logged payload equivalent). - zero-arg invocation logs just `vote` / `v` without trailing junk. These are the concrete assertion holes specifically relevant to direct interactive `zsh` `/vote` and `/v` behavior plus launcher correctness.
